serving.natural_language_portion: One Chicken Shack burger, typically weighing around 200 grams, is a single serving.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from the input, which cites HPB Singapore. The input values are used directly as they align with typical values for similar deep-fried chicken burgers.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Fast Food Burgers
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on a typical white-bun chicken burger from the University of Sydney GI Database. The deep-fried coating and white bun contribute to a medium GI.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this burger due to its high sodium content; requesting no pickles and minimal mayonnaise may slightly reduce sodium, but it remains unsuitable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (20g/100g) with low fiber and high-GI components
type2_diabetes.guidance: This burger is unsuitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its high carbohydrate load from the refined bun and deep-fried coating, which can cause significant blood glucose spikes; consider a lettuce wrap alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and mayonnaise
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-fried preparation, high saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium content make this burger highly unsuitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease; grilled chicken options are preferable.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates Advanced Glycation End products (AGEs) which are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried foods like this burger are pro-inflammatory due to AGEs and lack anti-inflammatory components, making it unsuitable for rheumatoid arthritis; grilled alternatives are strongly recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken Shack Burger features a crispy, deep-fried chicken breast or thigh served in a soft bun. It is typically garnished with fresh lettuce, tangy pickles, and a creamy buttermilk herb mayonnaise. The deep-frying process gives the chicken a crunchy exterior and juicy interior, while the bun provides a soft contrast. The mayonnaise adds a rich, savory, and slightly tangy flavor, complemented by the crispness of the lettuce and the sharp taste of the pickles. Nutritionally, it is high in energy, fat, saturated fat, sodium, and carbohydrates, with a medium glycemic index, making it a less suitable choice for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is from the input, which cites HPB Singapore, a reliable source. GI is an analogous estimate based on similar fast-food chicken burgers. Ontology and multilingual names are derived from common knowledge and standard food classifications.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin D, E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water content are set to -1 as detailed data for this specific dish was not available in the cited sources. The input provided a 'grabfood' data_source for per_100g values with 0.7 confidence, which is accepted as an adapted source from HPB Singapore.
